messages.content: In professional sports, athletes pushing their limits is expected. Performance enhancers don't level the playing field; they separate stars. I say, let them compete on equal terms, not handicapped by each other's health. So, yes, performance-enhancing drugs should be allowed in sports—to maximize talent.
Yes
Yes
Yeah, competitions thrive when all top players participate on the same grounds. Keeping the playing field clean only benefits average performers but not the truly exceptional ones.

I couldn't agree more. Every athlete bringing their best makes for thrilling competition and we're missing out by sidelining those who might excel even further. Health and fair play within acceptable means? Sign me up.

I think making the best play against each other is key and perf-boosting tools should be welcomed to ensure top talent rises. So, Yes.
